mapping.a_element: Lie algebras of low dimension used for classification of invariant PDEs
mapping.b_element: category-theoretic structure used to find simplest representatives of quotient objects (equations)

mapping.a_element: symmetry algebras of nonlinear heat-conductivity equations
mapping.b_element: symmetries of parabolic equations as morphisms in the category

mapping.a_element: group classification of PDEs via Lie algebra properties
mapping.b_element: classification of PDEs via morphisms and quotient objects in a category

mapping.a_element: maximal symmetry algebras of invariant evolution equations
mapping.b_element: simplest representatives of quotient objects in the category of parabolic equations

brief_justification: While both papers address classification problems for PDEs, Paper A uses the machinery of Lie algebras and symmetry groups to classify invariant equations, whereas Paper B employs category-theoretic morphisms and quotient objects. The underlying mathematical structures (Lie algebras vs. category theory) and mechanisms (symmetry analysis vs. categorical simplification) are fundamentally distinct.
